mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 4.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 4.0% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.16% as of June 2011 suggests potential easing trajectory
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134,468 thousand as of June 2011
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth is below 100k, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 2,000 thousand units in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,123 thousand and RSAFS at 357,841 million as of May 2011
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC difference > 0.05 in favor of univariate).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, indicating slowing manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as cyclical equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns is below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or directional accuracy is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth remains below 150,000 monthly, signaling a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer purchasing power and equity risk appetite, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.0% AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 150,000 in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% and PAYEMS at 134,468 thousand as of June 2011
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no significant explanatory power beyond univariate UNRATE (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, creating negative real rates that trigger equity market volatility and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.8 and FEDFUNDS at 4.16% as of May-June 2011
regime_hint: stagflation risk or inflation surprise with accommodative policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the real rate signal (FEDFUNDS minus inflation) shows no correlation with forward returns (|r| < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized, signaling a weakening economic expansion that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth multiples downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% in the most recent quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,202.3 billion (chained 2017 dollars) as of Q1 2011
failure_condition: Correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is positive or near-zero (|r| < 0.15), or SPX produces positive returns in more than 55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,800 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month, indicating a synchronized contraction in housing and consumption demand that signals recession risk and triggers equity market repricing downward over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,800 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,123 thousand and RSAFS at 357,841 million as of May 2011
regime_hint: demand shock or recession entry phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than HOUST alone (Rank IC difference > 0.04 in favor of univariate).
